**Lightforth**

**Product OKR / KPI (Q2) — Revenue-Focused**

*Product Manager  —  Adedamola Adewale (Moski)*

| Period: | Q2 2026 (April – June 2026\) |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Review Cadence:** | Weekly check-ins  |  Monthly reviews  |  End-of-quarter assessment |
| **Prepared by:** | Lightforth Product Team |
| **Approved by:** |  |
| **Deadline:** | Revised KPIs due COB Tuesday, May 5, 2026 |
| **Framework:** | Unified OKR \+ KPI Template — Thelix Holdings Group |

| OBJECTIVE: Drive Consistent Revenue Growth |
| :---- |
| *Focus: \+35% MoM revenue, paid user growth, freemium→paid conversion, pay-as-you-go adoption* |

| WEIGHT 30% | REVENUE CONTRIBUTION RATIONALE: Revenue growth is the primary objective for Lightforth in Q2. A 40–60% MoM increase in total revenue (NGN \+ USD) and a 20–30% improvement in freemium-to-paid conversion are direct and measurable financial targets for the business. |
| :---: | :---- |

| Ref | Key Results | Timeline | Metrics | Objective |
| :---: | ----- | :---: | ----- | ----- |
| **01** | **Revenue Growth**• Increase total quarterly revenue by **35%** • Increase paid users from **281 → 500** • Improve freemium → paid conversion from **2.0% → 5.5%** • Increase recurring subscription revenue contribution from **28% → 40%** | *Q2 2026 Monthly tracking* | • Current MRR (NGN \+ USD): **NGN440,750 \+ $859** • Paid users count: **281** • Conversion rate (Free → Paid): **5.5%** • Revenue split (NG vs INT): **21% & 79%** • Renewal revenue contribution(INT vs NG): **18% \+ 3%** | Drive measurable revenue growth by converting more users and improving retention quality. |

| OBJECTIVE: Improve Activation & First Value Experience |
| :---- |
| *Focus: Upload/tailor/paywall reach rate, reduce signup→completion drop-off, onboarding clarity* |

| WEIGHT 20% | REVENUE CONTRIBUTION RATIONALE: Users who experience core value quickly are significantly more likely to pay. Improving activation rate and reducing drop-off from sign-up to first completion directly feeds the conversion pipeline and increases paid user numbers. |
| :---: | :---- |

| Ref | Key Results | Timeline | Metrics | Objective |
| :---: | ----- | :---: | ----- | ----- |
| **02** | **Activation & First Value Experience** • Increase resume creation activation from **17/day → 100/day** • Increase onboarding completion rate from **42% → 70%** • Reduce onboarding drop-off from **58% → 30%** • Improve time-to-first-value from **12 mins → under 5 mins** | *Q2 2026*  | • Current daily signups: **25/day** • Current resumes created: **17/day** • Current onboarding completion: **42%** • Current drop-off rate: **58%** • Current time-to-first-value: **12 mins** | Ensure users quickly experience product value, increasing the likelihood of payment. |

| OBJECTIVE:  Optimize Conversion & Monetization Layer |
| :---- |
| *Focus: Paywall optimization, resume pay-per-download, subscription upgrades, checkout friction reduction* |

| WEIGHT 20% | REVENUE CONTRIBUTION RATIONALE: Converting intent into actual revenue requires a frictionless, well-structured paywall. Improving paywall conversion rate, payment success rate, and plan selection distribution directly increases the revenue generated per active user on the platform. |
| :---: | :---- |

| Ref | Key Results | Timeline | Metrics | Objective |
| :---: | ----- | :---: | ----- | ----- |
| **03** | **Conversion Optimization (Monetization Layer)**  • Improve paywall conversion from **2.0% → 6.0%** • Improve payment success rate from **78% → 92%** • Reduce checkout drop-off from **49% → 20%** • Increase pay-as-you-go resume purchases to **150+ monthly purchases** • Increase Pro/Premium upgrades by **40%** | *Q2 2026 Continuous experiments* | • Current paying customers: **281** • Current paywall conversion: **2.0%** • Current payment success rate: **78%** • Current checkout drop-off: **49%** | Turn more purchase intent into actual revenue by improving payment and upgrade flows. |

| OBJECTIVE: Deliver High-Quality Product & Market Fit |
| :---- |
| *Focus: 99.9% uptime, top 3 user-requested features, 15% bug reduction, CSAT/NPS* |

| WEIGHT 10% | REVENUE CONTRIBUTION RATIONALE: Platform reliability and feature relevance directly protect revenue. Downtime blocks payments and frustrates paying users. Implementing the top 3 user-requested features signals product-market fit and drives both retention and word-of-mouth acquisition. |
| :---: | :---- |

| Ref | Key Results | Timeline | Metrics | Objective |
| :---: | ----- | :---: | ----- | ----- |
| **04** | **Product Quality & Market Fit**  • Maintain **99.9% uptime** • Reduce critical production bugs from **8 → 0** • Launch top requested features: — Resume pay-as-you-go (Live) — Copilot desktop (Live)) — Copilot and Interview Prep Transcript (Live) — Chat with Resume — Resume Section Acceptance • Improve AI response speed from **4s → under 1.5s** | *Q2 2026 Continuous* | • Current uptime: **99.2%** • Current critical bug count: **8** • Current AI response latency: **4s** • Current CSAT: **N/A** • Current NPS: **N/A** | Deliver a high-quality product that improves user trust, usability, and retention. |

| OBJECTIVE: Improve Retention & Revenue Continuity |
| :---- |
| *Focus: Returning users NG (4%→10–15%), international renewal stability, and reducing refund rates* |

| WEIGHT 10% | REVENUE CONTRIBUTION RATIONALE: Moving from one-time payments to repeat revenue is the key to Lightforth's sustainable growth. Improving the NG returning user rate from 4% to 10–15% and reducing international refund rates directly increase revenue per user and reduce churn cost. |
| :---: | :---- |

| Ref | Key Results | Timeline | Metrics | Objective |
| :---: | ----- | :---: | ----- | ----- |
| **05** | **Retention & Revenue Continuity** • Increase NG returning users from **14% → 25%** • Increase International renewal rate from **28% → 45%** • Reduce refund rate from **12% → 5%** • Increase revenue per paying user from **$5 → $20 equivalent** | *Q2 2026 Continuous* | • Current NG returning users: **14%** • Current International renewal rate: **28%** • Current refund rate: **12%** • Current revenue per paying user: **\~$5 equivalent** | Move users from one-time payments to recurring revenue behaviour. |

| OBJECTIVE: Improve Growth & Acquisition Efficiency |
| :---- |
| *Focus: High-converting product flows, influencer campaigns, UGC creatives, and reducing CAC* |

| WEIGHT 5% | REVENUE CONTRIBUTION RATIONALE: Every naira and dollar spent on marketing must convert into paying users. Reducing CAC while improving activation per traffic source ensures marketing spend generates measurable revenue rather than vanity metrics. |
| :---: | :---- |

| Ref | Key Results | Timeline | Metrics | Objective |
| :---: | ----- | :---: | ----- | ----- |
| **06** | **Growth & Acquisition Efficiency** • Reduce CAC from **$31 → $18** • Improve campaign conversion rate from **1.8% → 4.5%** • Improve landing page conversion from **9% → 18%** • Launch more  influencer \+ UGC acquisition system at scale (Partners) | *Q2 2026 Continuous* | • Current CAC: **$31** • Current campaign conversion: **1.8%** • Current landing page conversion: **9%** • Current paid conversion from ads: **2.1%** | Ensure marketing spend translates into efficient paying-user acquisition. |

| OBJECTIVE  Accelerate Experimentation Velocity |
| :---- |
| *Focus: 8–12 product experiments per month, test copy/pricing/UX, implement winning variations fast* |

| WEIGHT 3% | REVENUE CONTRIBUTION RATIONALE: Rapid experimentation on onboarding, pricing, paywall, and payment flows directly improves conversion rates. Each winning experiment implementation compounds revenue gains — making experimentation velocity a direct revenue driver. |
| :---: | :---- |

| Ref | Key Results | Timeline | Metrics | Objective |
| :---: | ----- | :---: | ----- | ----- |
| **07** | **Experimentation Velocity** • Run **2-4 validated experiments monthly** • Maintain minimum experiment sample size of **500 users** • Maintain minimum experiment runtime of **5 days** • Ship winning experiment iterations within **7 days** |  *April – June 2026* | • Current experiments/month: **4** • Current experiment implementation speed: **21 days average** • Current validated experiment rate: **35%** | Improve product performance through measurable experimentation and iteration. |

| OBJECTIVE: Maintain Product Infrastructure Supporting Revenue |
| :---- |
| *Focus: Stable payments (Flutterwave, Stripe), credit allocation, AI reliability, minimal downtime, error monitoring* |

| WEIGHT 2% | REVENUE CONTRIBUTION RATIONALE: Payment infrastructure failures directly block revenue. Any downtime on Flutterwave or Stripe, credit allocation bugs, or AI reliability issues cause users to abandon payment flows — making technical infrastructure a direct revenue protection objective. |
| :---: | :---- |

| Ref | Key Results | Timeline | Metrics | Objective |
| :---: | ----- | :---: | ----- | ----- |
| **08** | **Product Infrastructure Supporting Revenue**  • Maintain payment uptime at **99.9%** • Reduce payment failures from **22% → 8%** • Reduce infrastructure incident resolution time from **9hrs → 1hr** • Mintain Dev ops (Digital Ocean) and stabilize production environment | *Q2 2026 Continuous* | • Current infrastructure uptime: **99.2%** • Current payment failure rate: **22%** • Current average incident resolution time: **9 hours** • Current active payment providers: **Flutterwave \+ Stripe** | Ensure infrastructure reliability does not block revenue generation or user trust. |

| REVENUE CONTRIBUTION WEIGHTING SUMMARY   |  |
| :---- | :---: |
| **Objective** | **Weight** |
| Objective 1 — Revenue Growth | **30%** |
| Objective 2 — Activation & First Value | **20%** |
| Objective 3 — Conversion & Monetization | **20%** |
| Objective 4 — Product Quality & Market Fit | **10%** |
| Objective 5 — Retention & Revenue Continuity | **10%** |
| Objective 6 — Growth & Acquisition Efficiency | **5%** |
| Objective 7 — Experimentation Velocity | **3%** |
| Objective 8 — Product Infrastructure | **2%** |
| **TOTAL** | **100%** |
